title: Anthropic drops ‘workplace AI agents’ directly inside Slack
slides: Can Anthropic agents inside Slack replace your team?
Claude now acts directly within Slack channels
It sees your messages but not your private DMs
You can assign it tasks right where you chat
Test one agent on a single project this week
thread_text:
1. AI that replaces your team?

Claude now acts directly inside Slack channels, seeing messages but not private DMs.

2. Assign tasks mid-chat

You don't need a separate interface — just type what you need where you already collaborate.

3. Privacy boundary set

Your private DMs stay off-limits, but anything in a public channel is fair game for Claude.

4. One project test drive

Slack suggests testing one agent on a single project this week, limiting disruption.

5. Will you test it?

Take one repetitive Slack project and assign it to Claude this week. Measure time saved vs. editing needed.
video_script: Claude now lives inside your Slack channels.
It reads messages but not your private DMs.
You can assign tasks right where you chat.
Test one agent on a single project.
Will it replace your team this week?

script: Claude now lives inside your Slack channels.
It reads messages but not your private DMs.
You can assign tasks right where you chat.
Test one agent on a single project.
Will it replace your team this week?

instagram.caption:
You can now assign tasks to Claude inside your Slack channels without leaving the chat. 🤖

Claude sees public messages but not private DMs. You type a request mid-conversation, and it acts on the project right there. No separate interface needed.

Slack suggests testing one agent on a single project this week. Your repetitive workflows could shrink to a command.

What repetitive task in your Slack would you hand off starting today?

carousel_slides.description: Claude now works inside Slack channels, processing messages but not private DMs.
No separate interface needed — type tasks where you already chat, without disruption.
Claude sees public channel messages only, keeping your private conversations off-limits.
Pick a single repetitive project and assign it to Claude — measure time saved.
Take one Slack task and assign it to Claude this week — link in bio.
